All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
AlreadyShotException
Thrown when a shot is attempted on a cell that has already been fired upon.
AppInitializer
Handles the application's bootstrapping sequence and initial stage preparation.
BattleShiException
Base checked exception for every domain-level error that the Battle Shi game can raise.
Board
Represents a 10x10 board: the grid of Cell objects and the fleet of Ship instances placed on it.
BoardGridView
Builds and owns the visual grid for a single 10x10 board: the column letters (A-J) and row numbers (1-10), the water cells themselves, and a transparent overlay layer where ships and shot markers are placed.
BoardSpriteLayer
Manages the PNG sprite ImageView overlays that sit on top of a BoardGridView's overlay layer.
BoardTheme
Centralizes every geometric measurement used to build board and ship shapes programmatically.
Cell
Represents a single cell on a board: its coordinate, its current visual/logical state, and (if any) the ship occupying it.
CellState
Represents the visual and logical state of a single board cell.
Coordinate
Immutable representation of a single board position.
FleetPlacementThread
Places the machine player's fleet on a background thread so the JavaFX Application Thread — and therefore the UI — is never blocked while the random placement algorithm runs.
FleetPlacementThread.PlacementReadyCallback
Callback invoked on the JavaFX Application Thread once the machine fleet has been placed (successfully or not).
GameController
Controller for game-view.fxml.
GamePersistenceManager
Handles all read and write operations for Battle Shi's two persistence files: Binary save file (battleshi_save.dat) — a Java serialized GameSession written to the user's home directory.
GameSession
Serializable snapshot of a complete, in-progress Battle Shi match.
GameState
Represents the overall phase the current match is in.
HumanPlayer
Represents the human player.
IllegalGameActionException
Thrown when an action that requires an active turn (e.g.
IMachineShotStrategy
Defines how the machine player decides where to shoot next.
InvalidPlacementException
Thrown when an attempt is made to place a ship in a position that violates placement rules: overlapping another ship, touching an adjacent ship, or falling outside the board boundaries.
MachinePlayer
Represents the computer-controlled opponent.
MachineTurnThread
Executes the machine player's turn on a dedicated background thread so the JavaFX Application Thread is never blocked during the artificial "thinking" delay.
MachineTurnThread.MachineActionCallback
Callback invoked on the JavaFX Application Thread once the machine has fired its shot and the model has been updated.
Main
Main application entry point for the runtime framework compilation layer.
MarkerShapeFactory
Builds the small overlay shapes drawn on top of a single cell to represent a shot's outcome: a miss (thick rounded white X) or a hit (solid red circle).
MenuController
Controller for menu-view.fxml, the application's true entry screen (see AppInitializer).
Orientation
Represents the orientation a ship can have on the board.
PersistenceException
Thrown when reading or writing persistence files fails, whether it is the serialized save game or the plain-text player data file.
PlacementController
Drives the ship-placement phase of a match on a single Board / BoardGridView pair.
PlacementController.Selection
Immutable-enough record of what is currently "in hand" while placing.
Player
Base class shared by every kind of player in the game.
RandomFleetPlacer
Populates a Board with a complete, valid fleet using a random placement algorithm.
RandomHuntStrategy
Machine shot strategy that combines a pure-random phase with a "hunt & target" phase: Random phase: when there is no active hit being pursued, the strategy picks a random cell that has not been shot at yet. Hunt phase: when the last shot was a CellState.HIT, the strategy enqueues the four orthogonal neighbors of that hit (filtering out out-of-bounds and already-shot cells) and then fires randomly among those candidates.
SceneManager
Singleton class responsible for managing the primary JavaFX Stage and handling scene transitions throughout the application.
Ship
Represents a single ship placed on a board: its type, orientation, the coordinates it occupies and which of those coordinates have already been hit.
ShipShapeFactory
Builds the JavaFX Group representing a ship's visual body.
ShipTrayView
Builds the fleet tray shown below the player's board, listing every ship in the fleet in the "shadow" style used for already-placed ships.
ShipType
Defines the four ship categories available in the fleet, along with the number of cells each ship occupies and how many units of that type must be placed on a single board.
Shot
Immutable record of a single shot fired during the match.
Shot.Shooter
Identifies which side fired the shot.
SpriteOverlayFactory
Loads PNG sprites from the classpath and creates ImageView overlays for ships and shot markers.
